violations.text: Allergen statement is lacking on menu. Per PIC, allergen statement will be added when menus are re-printed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment is to apply for a RICFSM with RIDOH. Application was printed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2026

Beans, cheese sauce, and gravy, which was previously cooked and cooled, was not reheated for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. All food was reheated to 165 or above at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

Collard greens and chicken wings, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Collard greens were observed at 75 DF and chicken wings were observed at 65 DF. All food was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, chicken wings and collard greens were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

Knives were observed being stored in between the prep unit and prep table. Utensils must be stored in a clean dry location. Knives were moved to 3-bay sink at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

Utensils spoons and forks in the kitchen area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026
2 door reach-in gasket was observed torn and in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. -Refrigeration units/shelves -Hot holding units -Ice machine Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application was given to PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
Bar cooler door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025
The condensate from the bar cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per interview with chef, he will be taking class next week. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) was observed with red and orange biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Sausage and pork was observed held at 118-130 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Cheese sauce was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Per interview with Chef, chicken wings are par cooked. A FOOD ESTABLISHMENT shall obtain a VARIANCE from the REGULATORY AUTHORITY for non-continuous cooking. Process to be ceased until approval is granted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
Bar cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025

Low boy refrigerator located in the kitchen area was not in good repair, observed temperature at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC took unit out of service at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the dry storage shelving have an accumulation of rodent droppings. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
The prep sink located in the kitchen was in poor repair. Per interview with PIC, service has been contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025
The condensate from the bar cooler is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/21/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 07/28/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

Crab meat, cheese, shrimp, etc. were observed held at 53-54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023

ROP salmon was observed thawed with packaging still in tact. PIC informed to open ROP packaging. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023
The 2 door reach-in located in the (rear) exit area was not in good repair. (Ambient 59 degrees F.) Unit taken out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 07/12/2023
The plumbing to the handsink was not in good repair. Leak observed. New Violation. Correct By: 07/21/2023
